brand.name: proof sealed and dropped
brand.logo_concept: ProofDrop's logo communicates tamper-evidence, cryptographic trust, and instant clarity. The mark combines a shield or drop shape with a checkmark — representing the "proof sealed and dropped" moment. The blue-600 primary color (#2563EB) grounds the identity in professional trust and technical precision, echoing financial services and legal-tech visual language.
brand.favicon_spec: 16×16 and 32×32: Just the shield-drop mark in blue-600 (#2563EB) on transparent background. For ICO file: include 16×16, 32×32, and 48×48. Apple touch icon (180×180): Full mark centered on white rounded background with 20px padding.

implementation_targets.task: Create a site-local logo component from the logo specification or logo-studio output.
Replace any shared generic brand mark with the site-local logo and brand copy.
Integrate the site-local logo, wordmark, and brand voice into the footer.
Wire translated tokens, typography, and global utility classes into the app stylesheet.
Apply the translated Tailwind theme so runtime styling matches the design system.
Persist the normalized design contract for downstream planning and validation.

validation_requirements: A site-local logo component must exist and replace any generic shared badge.
Navigation, footer, and layout surfaces must import the site-local brand assets.
Translated tokens and typography must be wired into runtime CSS and Tailwind config.
Phase 2 planning must consume this handoff file, not raw design docs alone.
